title: Devices, systems, and methods using microtransponders
abstract: An object may include at least one microtransponder (MTP) configured with an identifier. The identifier of the MTP may be indexed to the object. Indexing information associated with the MTP and the object may be stored in a database of a security system. The MTP may be read, and data reported by the MTP may be processed to determine authenticity of the object.
claims: 1. A method of protecting an electronic device comprising: fabricating the electronic device, the fabricating comprising attaching at least one optically powered microtransponder (MTP) to the electronic device, the MTP being configured with an identifier stored in a memory of the MTP, the MTP being sized at 2 mm×2 mm×0.2 mm or less, the MTP comprising a clock recovery circuit configured to be used for extracting the identifier and comprising: a photoconductor having a resistance configured to vary as a function of received light intensity, a reference resistor having a fixed resistance, the photoconductor and the reference resistor in a series combination forming a voltage divider such that a modulated light input incident on the photoconductor produces a modulated voltage signal; indexing the identifier stored in the memory of the MTP and configured to be extracted using the clock recovery circuit to the electronic device; and storing indexing information associated with the MTP and the electronic device in a database of a security system.
